tiers.name: low
tiers.label: Mechanical standardization
tiers.focus: Focus ONLY on these tasks — they are mechanical find-and-replace standardization: - Phase 4 (code quality): Inconsistent naming — find GetXAsync vs FetchXAsync vs LoadXAsync and standardize. Find string parameters that should be constants or nameof(). - Phase 4 (code quality): Magic strings — replace inline role strings with RoleNames.* constants, inline action names with nameof() - Phase 3 (views): Inline avatar tags — replace with @await Component.InvokeAsync("UserAvatar", ...) calls - Phase 3 (views): Icon consistency — replace any bi bi-* with fa-solid fa-* - Phase 2 (caching): Cache key strings — extract inline cache key strings to constants in a CacheKeys static class Skip all other tasks — they will be handled in later passes by a more capable model.

tiers.name: medium
tiers.label: Pattern consolidation
tiers.focus:
Focus ONLY on these tasks — they require tracing patterns across a few files:

- Phase 1 (controllers): Consolidate error handling — standardize on TempData["SuccessMessage"]/TempData["ErrorMessage"] pattern, create SetSuccess/SetError helpers on HumansControllerBase if not already present, migrate controllers
- Phase 2 (caching): Consolidate caching patterns — standardize on GetOrCreateAsync everywhere, replace TryGetValue + manual Set patterns
- Phase 4 (jobs): Standardize background job patterns — consistent method naming (ExecuteAsync), add structured logging at job start/end
- Phase 3 (email): Reduce EmailRenderer boilerplate — extract common render pattern if possible

Skip controller base class extraction and god class breakup — those need deeper analysis.

tiers.name: high
tiers.label: Architectural refactoring
tiers.focus:
Focus ONLY on these tasks — they require understanding cross-cutting patterns:

- Phase 1 (controllers): Extract HumansControllerBase — create base class with GetCurrentUserAsync, RequireCurrentUserAsync. Migrate controllers incrementally, verify build after each.
- Phase 1 (controllers): Extract team access helpers — the resolve-team-validate-access pattern used in TeamAdminController, ShiftAdminController, CampAdminController
- Phase 1 (controllers): Consolidate authorization — replace inline User.IsInRole() with [Authorize(Roles)] attributes where feasible
- Phase 2 (services): Move business logic out of controllers — extract LINQ transforms, ViewModel construction, and business decisions into services

Skip god class breakup (TeamService splitting) — that's the highest-risk refactor and needs its own pass.

tiers.name: super
tiers.label: High-risk structural changes
tiers.focus:
Focus ONLY on these tasks — they are high-risk structural refactors:

- Phase 2 (services): Break up TeamService.cs (1,639 lines) — extract TeamRoleService for role definitions + role assignments, create ITeamRoleService in Application layer, update DI registration, migrate callers
- Phase 2 (services): Break up TeamAdminController.cs (888 lines) — extract TeamRoleAdminController for role definition and role assignment endpoints
- Phase 5 (tests): Write tests for any new services, helpers, or base classes extracted in earlier passes
- Phase 6 (docs): Update feature docs in docs/features/ affected by refactoring

These are the riskiest changes. Verify build AND tests pass after every individual extraction. Commit frequently.
